free_text: Characterization and Application of 20-90 MeV Li-
7(P,N) Neutron Source at Tiara.
International Conference on
Nuclear Data For Science and Technology, May 9-13,
1994, Gatlinburg, Tn, Usa.
Data in Digital Form.
Thickness of Lithium Targets.
A Paper of M.Baba Et Al. Is to be Published in Nim/a.
More
Experimental
Details.
K-110 Avf Cyclotron at Tiara =
Takasaki Ion Accelerators For Radiation Application.
Metal Target of Lithium-7, 99.8% Isotopically Pure,
Was Used As a Neutron Source. The Target Thickness
Was:
3.6 mm For Incident Proton Energy of 43 MeV,
5.2 mm For Incident Proton Energy of 67 MeV.
- By Compiler - In More Recent Communications, the
Authors Give the Incident Proton Energy of 68 MeV
Instead of 67 MeV Given in the Primary Reference.
Double Differential Cross Sections For
Production of Secondary Protons, Deuterons and Tritons
In Carbon by 64-MeV Neutrons.
By Khlopin Radium Institute - A.P.
Data and Bib Sections are Corrected by F.C.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
Response of Na-I-
Tl Detector.
Recoil Protons from a Polyethylene Plate, 0.12-1.0
Mm Thick, 8*9 Cm, with a 6-Cm Diam. Hole in the
Centre.
Proton Recoil Telescope Consisting
Of a 5-Cm Diam. By 3-Cm Thick Na-I-Tl Crystal, and a
Transmission-Type Silicon Detector, was Placed at
About 5.5-M Distance from the Lithium Target. The
Radiation-Telescope Distance was About 40 Cm. The
Telescope was Shielded from the Neutron Beam by a
Brass Shadow Bar, 50-Cm Long, 5.5-Cm Diameter.
Ionization Chambers with U-238 and Th-232
Layers were Placed Around the Lithium Target and
Served For Relative Neutron Beam Monitoring.
Absolute Measurement of Proton Beam Current Using a
Faraday Cup.
Performed: Sample-Out and Carbon Backgrounds; Proton
Energy Loss in the Sample, Detector Elements, and the
Air Between the Radiator and the Detector; Energy
Dependence of the Detection Efficiency; Inelastic
Scattering of the Protons in the Detectors.
Neutron Fluence Data from Authors' Priv. Comm.
In Authors' Units: Neutron/Sr/MeV/Micro-Coulomb of the
Proton Beam.

- By Compiler - The Contents of Columns 'Data' and
'Data-Err' is a Result of Compiler'S Conversion from
Authors' Data to the Double Differential Cross
Sections For Neutron Emission.
The Converted Data are Normalized As to Obtain the
Angular Differential Cross Section Under the High-
Energy Peak Equal to the Value Given by Authors, See
Subent 003.
The Conversion Does not Take Into Account Energy
Dependence of the Corrections For Neutron Attenuation
In the Target, the Air and the Windows.
Data are Received from the Authors.
